One Apartments — Operational Management System
Case Study · Monday.com · Property Management

One Apartments — Centralised Operations Management System

Monday.com Workflow Automation CRM Short-Term Rental Property Management
A centralised operations management system built on Monday.com for One Apartments — a short-term rental company operating across Bangkok, Edinburgh, London, Manchester, and Phuket. The system consolidates maintenance tracking, guest experience management, and procurement across all properties into a single structured platform, replacing fragmented communication channels with accountable, trackable workflows.
The Problem

One Apartments operated across five cities with no centralised system for tracking issues, guest feedback, or procurement. Maintenance requests arrived via WhatsApp, email, and Airbnb messages. Guest complaints were untracked. Inventory management was entirely manual. There was no visibility into task ownership, resolution timelines, or cross-property performance.

The Solution

A Monday.com workspace was built to centralise all three operational departments — Maintenance, Guest Experience, and Procurement & Inventory — into structured boards with defined workflows, task ownership, status tracking, and automated notifications. All incoming requests from multiple channels are funnelled into a single source of truth.

System Breakdown
🔧 Maintenance System
  • Unique issue IDs (MAINT-001 format) for every report
  • Multi-view tracking: Kanban, Calendar, Gantt, Table
  • Priority levels, assigned staff, and cost tracking
  • Captures issues from WhatsApp, Email, and Airbnb
🏨 Guest Experience System
  • Guest IDs (GX-001) linked to properties and cities
  • Issue categorisation: Aesthetic, Cleanliness, Inventory, Complaint
  • Reported by field tracks source (Guest Review, Staff, Manager)
  • Status pipeline from Reported → In Progress → Fixed
📦 Procurement & Inventory
  • Item tracking with quantity, cost, and supplier data
  • Linked to property and department for accountability
  • Status tracking from Requested → Ordered → Delivered
  • Connected to Maintenance board for issue-triggered restocking
Key Features
  • Automated status notifications triggered on issue updates and task assignments
  • Multi-channel intake: WhatsApp, Email, Airbnb, Booking.com — all funnelled into one board
  • Custom dashboard showing open issues, in-progress tasks, and costs per property
  • Cross-board linking between Maintenance, Guest Experience, and Procurement
  • Kanban, Calendar, Gantt, and Table views for different management needs
  • Role-based task ownership — maintenance staff, cleaners, managers, and suppliers
  • Issues FORM for structured intake directly into the Monday.com board
Data Implementation

25 real maintenance issues were structured and loaded into the system to demonstrate real-world implementation. Each issue was assigned a unique ID, property, priority level, responsible staff member, cost estimate, and resolution status — providing a live demonstration of the system under operational conditions.

Tools Used
Monday.com (Primary) Monday Automations Monday Forms Monday Dashboards Multi-Channel Intake

About Me

Who is Christopher Onu?

I'm Christopher Ufedojo Onu, a CRM & AI Automation Specialist. I help businesses replace fragmented, manual processes with intelligent automated systems that run 24/7.

My work spans CRM platform architecture, AI voice agent deployment, workflow automation, and multi-platform integrations — designed to cut operational costs and increase revenue for B2B and B2C businesses.

Every system I build solves a specific business pain point — whether it's a scattered maintenance operation, an untracked lead pipeline, or a manual booking process.

What I Do

Services I Offer

End-to-end automation solutions built to scale your business without scaling your headcount.

01🗂️
CRM Architecture
